Operator Experience (detailed):
- Natalie J. Ramirez, President & CEO (also serving as Director of Operations)
  Address: 4120 W Gulf-to-Lake Highway, Suite 200, Lecanto, FL 34461
  Phone: (352) 555-0142
  Email: nramirez@suncoastemt.com
  Experience: 18 years in EMS, starting as an EMT-B in Hillsborough County in 2007, later becoming a paramedic in 2010. Served as field training officer for a large private ambulance provider in the Tampa Bay area from 2012–2016. Advanced to Operations Supervisor overseeing a fleet of 14 ALS units and approximately 60 field staff. In 2018, she transitioned to a regional director role with responsibility for contract compliance, QA/QI programs, and integration with county 9-1-1 dispatch systems. Natalie has extensive experience in interfacility transport, critical care ground transport coordination, and disaster response planning, including deployment during Hurricane Irma and Hurricane Michael. She holds current Florida Paramedic certification, ACLS, PALS, ITLS, and has completed FEMA ICS-100, 200, 700, and 800.

- Michael T. Hollings, Operations Manager
  Address: 1185 S Rock Crusher Road, Crystal River, FL 34429
  Phone: (352) 555-0198
  Email: mhollings@suncoastemt.com
  Experience: 12 years of EMS field and supervisory experience, including 8 years with a hospital-based ambulance service in Orlando, FL. Michael has managed daily deployment for up to 10 units, implemented electronic patient care reporting (ePCR) systems, and developed continuing education programs for EMTs and paramedics. He has a strong background in data-driven scheduling, response time analysis, and vehicle maintenance programs. Certifications include Florida Paramedic, ACLS, PALS, ITLS, and EVOC instructor.

Owner Experience (detailed):
Suncoast Emergency Medical Transport, Inc. was formed in 2023 by a group of EMS professionals and healthcare administrators to provide dedicated Basic and Advanced Life Support ground transport services in west-central Florida, with a particular focus on Citrus County and surrounding rural communities. Collectively, the ownership team has over 45 years of combined experience in EMS operations, hospital administration, and healthcare compliance. The company has already secured non-emergency interfacility transport agreements in neighboring counties (pending activation) and has invested in three Type III ambulances (two ALS-equipped, one BLS) meeting current State of Florida and Citrus County equipment and safety standards. The business has established policies for clinical quality assurance, employee training and recertification, HIPAA compliance, infection control (including detailed post-exposure protocols and PPE policies), and vehicle preventive maintenance. The company’s leadership has experience integrating with regional 9-1-1 centers, implementing CAD interfaces, and participating in multi-agency disaster drills. Suncoast EMT intends to offer 24/7 coverage for interfacility and scheduled transports initially, with capacity to assist in surge coverage for emergency 9-1-1 calls as permitted by county protocols.

In summary, both the operator and owner bring substantial, specific, and current experience in EMS operations, regulatory compliance, crew training, and coordination with county fire rescue and hospital systems. They have already begun outreach to local hospitals, skilled nursing facilities, and rehabilitation centers in Citrus County to align their transport protocols with existing discharge planning and continuity-of-care practices.

No single outside entity (corporation, partnership, or trust) owns more than 10% of the outstanding shares. The above-listed individuals collectively control 100% of the voting shares of Suncoast Emergency Medical Transport, Inc. There are no silent partners with operational control, and no current management agreements that delegate day-to-day EMS operational authority to any third-party company.
